title: triticum intermedium subsp. campestre
intro: Triticum intermedium subsp. campestre, commonly known as intermediate wheatgrass, is a subspecies of grass in the wheat family. It is a perennial grass that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia and is known for its medicinal and nutritional properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Triticum intermedium subsp. campestre is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its seeds are also consumed as a nutritious food source rich in fiber, vitamins, and minerals.
